name: Orbit 3
description: Analogue double-scroll chaos oscillator. Six phase-offset outputs describe a 3D orbit around two equilibrium points. Ranges from 2.4mHz to 22kHz with 1V/oct tracking; deterministic yet irregular modulation or audio.

controls.name: AUDIO/LOW Range Switch
controls.description: Selects audio VCO range or low frequency VCLFO range.
controls.detail: AUDIO: 22Hz–22kHz · LOW: 2.4mHz–180Hz · 1Hz at centre in LOW

controls.name: FREQUENCY
controls.description: Sets underlying oscillation rate that drives the chaos.

controls.name: FREQ. MOD.
controls.description: Polariser for the FREQ. CV input. Sets exponential FM depth and direction.

controls.name: DISTRIBUTION
controls.description: Widens the orbit paths. CW = orbits dwell longer near each equilibrium point before flipping.
controls.detail: Interacts with TAME/WILD to set scroll character

controls.name: DIST. MOD.
controls.description: Polariser for the DIST. CV input. Depth of distribution modulation.

controls.name: TAME/WILD Switch
controls.description: Tame: two distinct scrolls, more structured. Wild: scrolls blur, more irregular.
controls.detail: Flip to affect spending-time behaviour and scroll-flip rate

controls.name: EQUILIBRIUM POINTS − / +
controls.description: Two knobs set the two equilibrium point positions. CW = further from origin.
controls.detail: Asymmetric settings produce asymmetric attractors · affect output amplitudes (3–12Vpp)

controls.name: V/OCT Trimmer
controls.description: Front-panel trim for pitch tracking. Factory calibrated.
controls.detail: Target: 4 octaves between 0V and +4V · settings: wild, dist 9 o'clock, EPs max CW

inputs.name: V/OCT.
inputs.description: Exponential FM input at 1V/oct in audio range (0.66V/oct in low).

inputs.name: FREQ.
inputs.description: Second exponential FM input, scaled by FREQ. MOD. polariser.

inputs.name: DIST.
inputs.description: Distribution modulation input, scaled by DIST. MOD. polariser.

inputs.name: EP−
inputs.description: CV for the negative equilibrium point position.

inputs.name: EP+
inputs.description: CV for the positive equilibrium point position.

inputs.name: RESET
inputs.description: Rising edge resets the chaos core (hard-sync style). May flip oscillation direction → octave-down at audio rate.

outputs.name: +X
outputs.description: Chaos axis X. Near-sine in stable regimes.

outputs.name: EP
outputs.description: Gate output tracking which equilibrium is being orbited. +5V for positive scroll, 0V for negative. Pseudo-random gate in LOW; square sound source in audio.

behaviors.name: Double-scroll attractor
behaviors.description: Oscillation traces a 3D orbit around two equilibrium points. Depending on DISTRIBUTION/EP/TAME-WILD, behaviour ranges from stable periodic orbits to bistable cycling to full chaos. Deterministic — same settings yield same trajectories.

behaviors.name: Tame vs wild modes
behaviors.description: TAME: two clearly-separated scrolls, pitched oscillation with periodic scroll flips. WILD: scroll boundaries blur, scroll-changes become frequent and irregular, higher entropy.

behaviors.name: Scroll-flip gate
behaviors.description: EP toggles every time the orbit jumps from one scroll to the other. Use as pseudo-random clock divider in LOW mode, or as a square sub-oscillator in audio mode.

behaviors.name: Asymmetric attractor
behaviors.description: Setting the two equilibrium points to different positions tilts the attractor. Asymmetric scrolls have different amplitudes; one may dominate, biasing the EP gate duty cycle.

behaviors.name: Reset forces stable mode
behaviors.description: Feeding EP back into RESET locks the core into stable oscillation. Frequency knob sets pitch; DISTRIBUTION/EP knobs produce stepped pitch changes — a chaotic quantizer.

behaviors.name: Reset octave-down
behaviors.description: RESET can flip oscillation direction on each edge. At audio rate this produces octave-down sync artefacts, unlike conventional hard sync's timbral shift.

behaviors.name: 60° phase bank
behaviors.description: Successive outputs are 60° apart. In stable regime, provides a six-phase sine bank for chorus/phaser-style patches; in chaos, six correlated-but-distinct modulation streams.

behaviors.name: Wide audio range
behaviors.description: Temperature-compensated 1V/oct tracking over at least 5 octaves. Usable as a playable VCO whose timbre morphs with DISTRIBUTION/EP between near-sine and noise.

patchIdeas.name: Slow chaotic modulation bus
patchIdeas.patch: LOW range · TAME · moderate DISTRIBUTION · six outputs drive six destinations (filter, VCA, pan, FX, etc.) · correlated-yet-different modulations give organic motion without six separate LFOs
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: LOW
* TAME/WILD: TAME
* DISTRIBUTION: noon

- Orbit 3 (+X) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Orbit 3 (+Y) >> VCA (CV)
- Orbit 3 (+Z) >> Pan (CV)
- Orbit 3 (−X) >> FX (Time)
- Orbit 3 (−Y) >> VCO (FM)
- Orbit 3 (−Z) >> Filter (Res)

patchIdeas.name: Chaotic duet via EP
patchIdeas.patch: LOW range · +Z → VCO FM (chaotic pitch drift) · EP gate → signal switch · switch alternates between two voice waveforms per scroll flip · each equilibrium hosts its own timbre for a call-and-response duet
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: LOW
* TAME/WILD: TAME

- Orbit 3 (+Z) >> VCO (FM)
- Orbit 3 (EP) g> Switch (CV)
- VCO (Saw) -> Switch (In A)
- VCO (Square) -> Switch (In B)
- Switch (Out) -> Filter (In)

patchIdeas.name: Saw-wave chaos VCO
patchIdeas.patch: AUDIO range · 1V/oct sequencer → V/OCT · +X → FREQ. input at max FREQ. MOD. · feedback warps the sine core · Z outputs become saw/ramp-like · chaotic overtones bend with DISTRIBUTION
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: AUDIO
* FREQ. MOD.: max

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Orbit 3 (V/OCT.)
- Orbit 3 (+X) >> Orbit 3 (FREQ.) // self-FM feedback
- Orbit 3 (+Z) -> Filter (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Stepped tone quantizer
patchIdeas.patch: AUDIO range · TAME · EP → RESET self-patch forces stable oscillation · sweep DISTRIBUTION or EP knobs · perceived pitch jumps in stepped intervals — a deterministic melodic quantizer without a quantizer module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: AUDIO
* TAME/WILD: TAME

- Orbit 3 (EP) t> Orbit 3 (RESET) // forces stable
- LFO (Out) >> Orbit 3 (DIST.)
- Orbit 3 (+Z) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Pseudo-random gate stream
patchIdeas.patch: LOW range · WILD · moderate speed · EP output streams irregular 0/5V gates · use as rhythmic trigger or section switcher · modulate DIST to vary density
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: LOW
* TAME/WILD: WILD

- Orbit 3 (EP) g> Drum (Trig)
- LFO (Out) >> Orbit 3 (DIST.)
- Orbit 3 (+Y) >> Drum (Pitch)

patchIdeas.name: Six-phase chorus/phaser
patchIdeas.patch: AUDIO range · TAME · low DISTRIBUTION · near-stable six-phase sine bank · mix pairs of ± outputs through VCAs and sum to approximate vintage 6-phase chorus · small drift gives natural motion
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: AUDIO
* TAME/WILD: TAME
* DISTRIBUTION: low

- Orbit 3 (+X) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Orbit 3 (+Y) -> Mixer (In 2)
- Orbit 3 (+Z) -> Mixer (In 3)
- Orbit 3 (−X) -> Mixer (In 4)
- Orbit 3 (−Y) -> Mixer (In 5)
- Orbit 3 (−Z) -> Mixer (In 6)
- Mixer (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Chaotic S&H source
patchIdeas.patch: LOW range · WILD · +X feeds S&H input · clock samples at steady rate · resulting voltages are correlated-but-irregular · feed to quantizer for deterministic melodic sequences
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: LOW
* TAME/WILD: WILD

- Orbit 3 (+X) >> S&H (In)
- Clock (Out) c> S&H (Trig)
- S&H (Out) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Oscilloscope XY art
patchIdeas.patch: AUDIO range · send any output pair to scope XY inputs · real-time Lissajous-style chaos figures · tweak DISTRIBUTION/EP/TAME-WILD to sculpt the visual attractor · musical AND visual module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Orbit 3:
* Range: AUDIO
* TAME/WILD: WILD

- Orbit 3 (+X) -> Scope (X)
- Orbit 3 (+Y) -> Scope (Y)
